health.s: 3 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the metal lapel pin contains high levels of lead.

topHazards.label: The metal lapel pin contains high levels of lead. Lead is toxic if ingested by young children and can cause adverse health effects.

sampleRecalls.title: Nintendo Recalls Lapel Pins Due to Risk of Lead Exposure
sampleRecalls.description: The recalled lapel pins have images depicting 12 video game characters. The lapel pins vary in size, ranging from 1 to 2 inches in height. The characters Diddy Kong, Donkey Kong, Kirby, Mario, Mario Kart, Pikachu, Princess Peach, Samus, Starfox, Waluigi and Wario were sold individually. Mario, Princess Peach and Samus…
hazards: The metal lapel pin contains high levels of lead. Lead is toxic if ingested by young children and can cause adverse health effects.

sampleRecalls.title: Nintendo of America Initiates Replacement Program for Wrist Straps Used with Controllers for the Wii Video Game System
sampleRecalls.description: The wrist straps are sold with Nintendo's Wii video game system (pronounced "we"). Its controller, called the Wii Remote, is shaped like a TV remote. Sensors determine the Wii Remote's position in 3-D space, which means that a tennis swing, for example, is done through movement of a consumer's hand rather than by just…
hazards: If consumers swing the hand-held "Wii Remote" game controllers using excessive force and accidentally let go, the cord connecting the controller to the wrist s…
injuries: Nintendo has received reports of cords on wrist straps breaking, including three reports of minor injuries not requiring medical attention. All of these incidents occurred when consumers were playing…
products: Wrist Straps Used with Controllers for Wii Video Game System

sampleRecalls.description: Nintendo Settlement with New York Attorney General NEWS RELEASE March, 2000 Alert #00-500 Nintendo Settlement with New York Attorney General The New York Attorney General announced a settlement with Nintendo of America, Inc. to protect children from hand injuries sustained while playing the "Mario Party" video game.
